2603.21970.title: Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning for Medical Text Summarization: A Comparative Study of Lora, Prompt Tuning, and Full Fine-Tuning
2603.21970.paper_summary: Fine-tuning large language models for domain-specific tasks such as medical text summarization demands substantial computational resources. Parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) methods offer promising alternatives by updating only a small fraction of parameters. This paper compares three adaptation approaches-Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA), Prompt Tuning, and Full Fine-Tuning-across the Flan-T5 model family on the PubMed medical summarization dataset. Through experiments with multiple random seeds, we demonstrate that LoRA consistently outperforms full fine-tuning, achieving 43.52 +/- 0.18 ROUGE-1 on Flan-T5-Large with only 0.6% trainable parameters compared to 40.67 +/- 0.21 for full fine-tuning. Sensitivity analyses examine the impact of LoRA rank and prompt token count. Our findings suggest the low-rank constraint provides beneficial regularization, challenging assumptions about the necessity of full parameter updates. Code is available at https://github.com/eracoding/llm-medical-summarization
2603.21970.paper_summary_zh: 微調大型語言模型以應對特定領域任務，如醫療文本摘要，需要大量的計算資源。參數高效微調（PEFT）方法通過僅更新一小部分參數，提供了有前景的替代方案。本文比較了三種適應方法——低秩適應（LoRA）、提示調整和完全微調——在Flan-T5模型系列上針對PubMed醫療摘要數據集的表現。通過對多個隨機種子的實驗，我們證明LoRA在性能上始終優於完全微調，在Flan-T5-Large上達到了43.52 +/- 0.18 ROUGE-1，而可訓練參數僅佔0.6%，相比之下完全微調的結果為40.67 +/- 0.21。敏感性分析檢查了LoRA秩和提示標記數量的影響。我們的研究結果表明，低秩約束提供了有益的正則化，挑戰了對於完全參數更新必要性的假設。代碼可在 https://github.com/eracoding/llm-medical-summarization 獲得。
2603.21970.author: Ulugbek Shernazarov et.al.
2603.21970.authors: Ulugbek Shernazarov, Rostislav Svitsov, Bin Shi
